ablations_and_sensitivity: Base model dependency: Kernels from a pretrained base model show ~10% higher performance and better conditioning than those from a distilled-data-only model (Figure 1).
Algorithm 1 is sensitive to thresholds τ_v and τ_g, with Pareto-optimal configurations being dataset-specific (Section 5.3, Figure 9).
baselines_and_comparisons: Comparison of Algorithm 1 against leverage, k-means, random, and FPS sampling showing that DNTK maintains accuracy/fidelity at much higher compression ratios (Figure 3/6).
Analysis of DNTK against exact NTK results (Figure 1) and random projection baselines (Section 6).
discussion_signals: Identification of a 'Guarantee Gap': Theorem 3.3 is a one-step bound, not a global predictive guarantee (Comment a334f9ac).
The 'Inductive Bias Gap': Distinction between geometric preservation and generalization preservation (Comment d4842375).
Discussion of OOD spectral conflict gaps (Comment 7cb030ac).
experimental_setup: Baselines: Leverage score, k-means, random sampling, and Farthest Point Sampling (FPS).
Metrics: Test accuracy, Test fidelity (prediction matching with full NTK), Test MSE, and kernel conditioning (λ_min and condition number).
key_assumptions: Lazy training regime where the network operates near a fixed reference parameter state (Section 3.1).
Property A: Local variance of kernel clusters is almost entirely contained within the global eigenspace (Section 3.4).
Property B: Local eigenspaces collectively do not span the global eigenspace, necessitating the capture of 'gap directions' (Section 3.4).
Availability of a high-quality pretrained model to perform initial distillation (Section 5.1).
likely_accept_arguments: Unprecedented 10^5x reduction in NTK complexity enabling previously intractable analyses.
Brilliant conceptual shift using distillation as a basis for kernel approximation.
Technically rigorous identification and solution for the local-global spectral gap.
likely_reject_arguments: Practical utility limited by pretraining requirements and high distillation overhead.
Heuristic reliance on spectral bias (label-blind optimization).
limitations_and_failure_modes: Dependency on high-quality pretrained models restricts utility to post-hoc analysis (Comment a334f9ac).
Spectral matching is 'label-blind' and may discard low-variance but high-signal directions (Comment d4842375).
Computational 'Catch-22': The cost of initial distillation is significant (Comment 623ab939).
main_contributions: Formalization of NTK redundancy at three levels: data, parameters, and gradient subspace (Definitions 3.1 and 3.2).
A theoretical framework linking dataset distillation to Nyström-like inducing point selection in the tangent-feature space (Theorem 3.3).
The DNTK pipeline, a three-stage compression framework (data, parameter, and gradient distillation).
Algorithm 1 (Local-Global Gradient Distillation), which explicitly synthesizes gradients to capture the 'local-global gap' in kernel spectra.
Empirical evidence of five orders of magnitude reduction in computation and storage while preserving predictive performance.
method_summary: DNTK applies three successive reductions: (1) Dataset distillation (WMDD) to compress the input dimension n to m; (2) Johnson-Lindenstrauss (JL) random projection to reduce the parameter dimension P to k; and (3) Local-Global Gradient Distillation (Algorithm 1) which partitions the tangent space into clusters and synthesizes a set of s << m gradients that capture both intra-cluster variance and the inter-cluster 'gap directions' (global principal directions poorly represented by local clusters).
novelty_signals: Inversion of the DD-NTK relationship: using distillation to accelerate kernel analysis rather than using kernels for distillation (Comment be2b5d4f).
Formalization of the 'local-global gap' (Property B) in tangent spaces.
Synthesis of 'gap representatives' to maintain spectral fidelity.
one_sentence_claim: The paper introduces the Distilled Neural Tangent Kernel (DNTK), which reduces NTK computational complexity by up to five orders of magnitude by combining dataset distillation, random projection, and a novel local-global gradient distillation algorithm.
red_flags: Theoretical disconnect between local one-step bounds and global predictive claims (Comment a334f9ac).
Post-hoc efficiency paradox: The method requires a fully trained model to achieve the speedup (Comment 623ab939).
reproducibility_signals: Complete pseudocode for Local-Global Gradient Distillation provided (Algorithm 1).
Complexity profiles for various sampling methods compared (Table 1).
Detailed hyperparameter grid search results (τ_v, τ_g, H) included (Figure 9).
score_relevant_quotes: "NTK computational complexity by up to five orders of magnitude while preserving kernel structure and predictive performance." (Page 1, Abstract)
"Theorem 3.3 is explicitly a one-step smoothness regret bound... it does not yet read as a direct guarantee for the full WMDD + JL + gradient-synthesis pipeline" (Comment 801d5b92)
"The method's effectiveness is heavily contingent on the availability of a high-quality pretrained model." (Comment a334f9ac)
"Roughly ε = 12-15% of global structure is not captured by the union of local clusters at the truncation rank." (Page 8, Figure 4)
statistical_reporting: 12-15% of global variance identified as 'coverage gap' not captured by local clusters (Figure 4).
Truncation rank of 32 at 95% explained variance for 'tench' class (Figure 4).
strongest_results: Reduction in NTK computational complexity and storage by up to five orders of magnitude (Section 1).
Algorithm 1 achieves 76% accuracy and 78% fidelity on ImageNette with only 5 distilled gradients, substantially outperforming sampling baselines (Section 5.3).
Class kernels exhibit (12, 0.05)- to (16, 0.05)-data redundancy, with truncation ranks between 31 and 41 (Section 5.2).
title: Efficient Analysis of the Distilled Neural Tangent Kernel
